section.name: tpm-plugins
section.summary: tmux Plugin Manager (TPM) installation, essential plugin configuration, and common pitfalls.

rule.statement: Install TPM before adding any plugin declarations to .tmux.conf; otherwise tmux.conf fails to source and all settings are lost.
rule.rationale: TPM's run directive at the bottom of .tmux.conf initializes all plugins. If the TPM directory does not exist, the run command silently fails and no plugins load.

example.code:
# Install TPM (one-time, before first use) git clone https://github.com/tmux-plugins/tpm ~/.tmux/plugins/tpm
# In .tmux.conf — declare plugins set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tpm' set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-resurrect' set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-continuum'
# MUST be the last line in .tmux.conf run '~/.tmux/plugins/tpm/tpm'
# After adding plugins: press prefix + I to install # To update: prefix + U

list.item: tmux-resurrect — saves and restores sessions including pane layout, running commands, and vim sessions
tmux-continuum — auto-saves sessions every 15 minutes; auto-restores on tmux server start
tmux-yank — copies to system clipboard over SSH (requires xclip/xsel/pbcopy on the client)

rule.statement: Enable @resurrect-capture-pane-contents so the text visible in each pane is saved and restored, not just the pane structure.
rule.rationale: Without pane contents capture, a restored session shows empty panes. With it, the last visible output (logs, process output) is replayed into each pane.

example.code:
set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-resurrect' set -g @plugin 'tmux-plugins/tmux-continuum'
# Resurrect: save pane content and vim session files set -g @resurrect-capture-pane-contents 'on' set -g @resurrect-strategy-vim 'session'
# Continuum: auto-save every 15 min, auto-restore on start set -g @continuum-restore 'on' set -g @continuum-save-interval '15'
# Manual save/restore commands (outside of auto): # prefix + Ctrl+s = save # prefix + Ctrl+r = restore

list.item: Forgetting prefix + I after adding a new plugin declaration — plugin directories are not created until install is run
TPM run directive not at the bottom of .tmux.conf — plugins declared after run do not load
tmux-yank requires xclip or xsel on the remote host for SSH clipboard forwarding to work
tmux-continuum auto-restore only runs when tmux server starts, not on attach; a running server will not auto-restore
